page: Amazon Web Services m8g.xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard workloads. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yield scores of 1707.29 and 6818.16, respectively. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.02 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.39 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. Single-threaded compression tasks show strong performance, with gzip compression reaching 30.34 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.17 GB/sec. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance bottleneck, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the poor category at 753.29. Large language model inference speeds are also limited, with text generation for a 7B model reaching 6.51 tokens per second.

The m8g.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose applications. Its dedicated arm64 cores provide consistent execution without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances, making it a cost-efficient choice for steady-state workloads. Given its benchmark profile, this server is well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate text processing tasks. It is less optimal for intensive cryptographic operations or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the arm64-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 4 dedicated physical cores at 2.8 GHz and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average capabilities across standard computing tasks, with strong single-threaded compression speeds of 30.34 GB/sec for gzip. However, cryptographic operations are a notable bottleneck, as evidenced by a poor PassMark encryption score. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for steady-state workloads requiring dedicated resources without burstable limits. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for steady-state web serving and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated arm64 general-purpose instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for steady-state web serving and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor with 4 dedicated cores
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Delivers 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Weak performance in cryptographic operations and large language model inference
Optimal for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
